beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, and light stretching at home.
beats.reasoning: Monday's energy was high and social; today's grey drizzle and SR-520 closure call for a slower, more intentional start. Felix wakes early but stays grounded at home before heading out.

beats.activity: Commute to Redmond studio via I-405 (avoiding SR-520 eastbound closure; extra 15 min buffer for rain).
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon, so Felix leaves early to arrive before the closure window peaks, taking the safer I-405 corridor despite rain adding 10–15 min to travel time.

beats.activity: Teach morning vinyasa flow class at the studio.; Reset and prep between classes; light admin, hydration, snack.; Teach restorative yoga and meditation class.
beats.reasoning: Regular early-morning class slot; steady rain outside makes the warm, indoor studio a natural draw for students seeking comfort and grounding.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at a nearby food truck or casual spot; rain keeps it quick and indoors.
beats.reasoning: Rain from 7am–2pm means outdoor food trucks are less appealing; Felix ducks into Redmond Town Center for a quick, dry meal before afternoon sessions.

beats.activity: Return to studio; prep for afternoon private sessions.; Teach one-on-one wellness coaching and personalized yoga session.
beats.reasoning: Short walk back; Felix uses this time to set up the private session space and catch up on client notes before the afternoon grind.

beats.activity: Commute back to Kirkland; rain has cleared, SR-520 eastbound now open again.
beats.reasoning: Rain cleared by 2pm and SR-520 closure lifted at noon; Felix can now take the direct route home, arriving with time to rest before evening plans.

beats.activity: Thrift store browsing and errand run (Goodwill or local vintage shop in Kirkland).
beats.reasoning: Post-rain afternoon is ideal for thrifting; Felix loves the hunt and found a great Patagonia fleece yesterday, so the mood carries over today.

beats.activity: Dinner and casual hangout with a close friend; grab a meal and chat.
beats.reasoning: Yesterday's social energy was high; Felix values time with close friends over crowds. A low-key dinner in Kirkland keeps the evening grounded and local.
(auto) ensure agent ends day at home

diary: Started the day in that gray-wash light, rain pattering against the windows. My usual morning ritual—stretching helped settle my mind before heading out. The SR-520 closure meant taking I-405, which felt longer than it should, but I'd padded the time. Teaching back-to-back classes at the studio kept me grounded; there's something about guiding people through their breath when the weather's dreary that feels necessary. The vinyasa flow had good energy despite everything. Grabbed lunch quick at the food truck near Redmond Town Center—rain made everyone hurry. My private session client seemed stressed, so we slowed things down, focused on grounding. Drive home was smooth once the bridge reopened. Found a vintage linen jacket at Goodwill that I didn't know I needed. Dinner with Maya at Molly Moon's felt like a relief—just laughed about nothing in particular. Home now, tired in the good way.
thought_process: The rain shaped everything today, honestly. I knew about the bridge closure early, so I mentally prepped an alternate route and gave myself buffer time—no point arriving frazzled before teaching wellness. The gloomy weather usually makes people anxious, so I leaned into longer holds and breathing work in class. At lunch, I wanted to move fast, in and out, which isn't my usual style, but the rain had that effect on everyone. By the afternoon session, I noticed my client carrying tension from their day, so I abandoned the planned sequence and just listened—sometimes that's the real coaching. The thrift store run was pure self-care; I needed something tactile and unstructured after a full teaching day. Dinner with Maya was exactly what I needed to decompress before heading home. The weather cleared by evening, which felt symbolic—like the day's weight lifting.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
